description_long: This object is a double-barreled shotgun with a classic side-by-side barrel configuration, featuring two long, parallel black barrels that dominate its length. The barrels are smooth and metallic, with a subtle sheen, and are joined together along their entire length. The receiver and trigger area are compact, with a visible hammer mechanism and a short, curved wooden grip that appears ergonomically shaped for one-handed use. The grip is made of a medium-brown wood, contrasting with the dark metal of the barrels and receiver. There is no visible stock, making the weapon more compact and likely intended for close-quarters use. The overall design is utilitarian, with minimal ornamentation and a focus on function.
description: A compact, side-by-side double-barreled shotgun with long black barrels and a short, curved wooden grip. The design is functional and lacks a full stock.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the twin black barrels and the short, curved wooden grip.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the two parallel barrel openings and the symmetry of the shotgun's muzzle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again displaying the long barrels and the wooden grip, with the hammer and trigger area visible.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the wooden grip and the back of the receiver, with the barrels extending away.
attribute_barrel_configuration: side-by-side double-barrel, smooth and parallel with no visible ribbing or venting
attribute_trigger_guard_design: rounded, medium-sized trigger guard with no visible finger grooves or reinforcement
